info.description:
OpenAPI schema for ROSA Regional Platform API generated from Go types with markers

Fields marked with +k8s:openapi-gen=false are excluded from this schema.
info.title: ROSA Regional Platform API

Cluster.description:
Cluster represents a HyperFleet managed OpenShift cluster
+kubebuilder:object:root=true
+kubebuilder:subresource:status
+kubebuilder:resource:scope=Namespaced

ClusterConfiguration.description:
ClusterConfiguration specifies configuration for individual OCP components in the cluster.
This is a HyperFleet-owned mirror of hypershiftv1beta1.ClusterConfiguration that allows
us to add granular markers to nested fields like kubelet config.

kubelet.description:
kubelet contains the configuration for kubelet on nodes.
This is where we can add granular control over kubelet fields.
+hyperfleet:write-mode=service-set

machineConfig.description:
machineConfig contains the configuration for machine-level settings (kernel params, systemd, files).
Granular markers allow safe subset exposure while hiding dangerous operations.
+hyperfleet:write-mode=service-set

ClusterList.description:
ClusterList contains a list of Clusters
+kubebuilder:object:root=true

name.description:
Name is the name of the Cluster resource
+kubebuilder:validation:Required

namespace.description:
Namespace is the namespace of the Cluster resource
If empty, defaults to the same namespace as this NodePool

ClusterSpec.description: ClusterSpec defines the desired state of a Cluster

cloudUrl.description:
CloudUrl is the CloudFront URL for accessing the cluster console (auto-populated by server)
+k8s:openapi-gen=true
+hyperfleet:write-mode=service-set

deleteProtection.description:
DeleteProtection prevents accidental deletion when enabled
+hyperfleet:write-mode=mutable

displayName.description:
DisplayName is a human-readable name for the cluster
+hyperfleet:write-mode=mutable
+kubebuilder:validation:MaxLength=256

expirationTimestamp.description:
ExpirationTimestamp marks when this cluster should be automatically deleted
+hyperfleet:write-mode=mutable

hostedCluster.description:
HostedCluster contains the full HyperShift HostedCluster configuration
All fields are generated from upstream and have safe defaults (hidden + service-set)
until explicitly reviewed and exposed

placement.description:
Placement is the management cluster name (auto-populated if not provided)
+hyperfleet:write-mode=mutable

properties.description:
Properties are arbitrary key-value pairs for customer metadata
+hyperfleet:write-mode=mutable

tags.description:
Tags are customer-defined labels for organizational purposes
This is a TechPreview feature
+hyperfleet:write-mode=mutable
+openshift:enable:FeatureGate=HyperFleetAutoScaling

ClusterStatus.description: ClusterStatus defines the observed state of a Cluster

apiEndpoint.description: APIEndpoint is the cluster API server endpoint

consoleUrl.description: ConsoleURL is the web console URL

readyTime.description: ReadyTime is when the cluster became ready

state.description:
State represents the high-level cluster state
+kubebuilder:validation:Enum=pending;provisioning;ready;degraded;deleting;failed

version.description: Version is the observed OpenShift version

FileSpec.description: FileSpec represents a file to write to nodes.

group.description: group is the file owner group

overwrite.description: overwrite specifies whether to overwrite existing files

path.description: path is the absolute path where the file should be written

user.description: user is the file owner user

HostedClusterSpecPassthrough.description: HostedClusterSpecPassthrough mirrors HostedClusterSpec from upstream HyperShift

autoNode.description:
autoNode specifies the configuration for automatic node provisioning and lifecycle management.
+k8s:openapi-gen=true
+hyperfleet:write-mode=service-set

configuration.description:
configuration specifies configuration for individual OCP components in the
+k8s:openapi-gen=true
+hyperfleet:write-mode=service-set

KubeletConfig.description:
KubeletConfig specifies kubelet configuration.
This is a HyperFleet-owned type that mirrors hypershiftv1beta1.KubeletConfig
with granular markers for customer control.

containerLogMaxFiles.description:
containerLogMaxFiles is the maximum number of container log files.
+hyperfleet:write-mode=mutable

containerLogMaxSize.description:
containerLogMaxSize is the maximum size of container log file before it is rotated.
+hyperfleet:write-mode=mutable

imageGCHighThresholdPercent.description:
imageGCHighThresholdPercent is the disk usage percent triggering image GC.
+hyperfleet:write-mode=mutable

imageGCLowThresholdPercent.description:
imageGCLowThresholdPercent is the disk usage percent to gc to.
+hyperfleet:write-mode=mutable

imageMinimumGCAge.description:
imageMinimumGCAge is the minimum age for an unused image before it is garbage collected.
+hyperfleet:write-mode=mutable

kubeReserved.description:
kubeReserved specifies resources reserved for Kubernetes system components.
+hyperfleet:write-mode=immutable

maxPods.description:
maxPods is the maximum number of pods per node.
Customers can set this to optimize for high-density workloads.
+hyperfleet:write-mode=mutable

podPidsLimit.description:
podPidsLimit is the maximum number of PIDs allowed per pod.
Customers can increase this for applications that spawn many processes.
+hyperfleet:write-mode=mutable

registryBurst.description:
registryBurst is the maximum size of bursty pulls, temporarily allows pulls to burst.
+openshift:enable:FeatureGate=HyperFleetKubeletAdvanced
+hyperfleet:write-mode=mutable

registryPullQPS.description:
registryPullQPS is the limit of registry pulls per second.
+openshift:enable:FeatureGate=HyperFleetKubeletAdvanced
+hyperfleet:write-mode=mutable

serializeImagePulls.description:
serializeImagePulls when enabled, tells kubelet to pull images one at a time.
Tech preview feature for optimizing image pull performance.
+openshift:enable:FeatureGate=HyperFleetKubeletAdvanced
+hyperfleet:write-mode=mutable

streamingConnectionIdleTimeout.description:
streamingConnectionIdleTimeout is the maximum time a streaming connection can be idle.
+hyperfleet:write-mode=mutable

systemReserved.description:
systemReserved specifies resources reserved for system daemons.
Customers can set this on cluster creation but cannot change it later.
+hyperfleet:write-mode=immutable

MachineConfigSpec.description:
MachineConfigSpec specifies machine-level configuration.
This controls kernel parameters, systemd units, and file writes.
Most fields are platform-managed for security and stability.

allowedKernelArguments.description:
allowedKernelArguments specifies kernel parameters customers can request.
This is a WHITELIST approach - customers can only request known-safe parameters.
Platform validates against an allowlist and applies approved parameters.
Tech Preview feature requiring explicit enablement.
+openshift:enable:FeatureGate=HyperFleetMachineConfig
+hyperfleet:write-mode=immutable

fips.description:
fips enables FIPS mode on nodes.
Immutable - must be set at cluster creation, cannot be changed.
+hyperfleet:write-mode=immutable

NodePool.description:
NodePool represents a HyperFleet managed NodePool for a cluster
+kubebuilder:object:root=true
+kubebuilder:subresource:status
+kubebuilder:resource:scope=Namespaced

NodePoolList.description:
NodePoolList contains a list of NodePools
+kubebuilder:object:root=true

NodePoolSpec.description: NodePoolSpec defines the desired state of a NodePool

autoRepair.description:
AutoRepair enables automatic repair of unhealthy nodes
+hyperfleet:write-mode=mutable

displayName.description:
DisplayName is a human-readable name for the node pool
+hyperfleet:write-mode=mutable

labels.description:
Labels to apply to nodes in this pool
+hyperfleet:write-mode=mutable

nodePool.description:
NodePool contains the full HyperShift NodePool configuration
All fields are generated from upstream and have safe defaults (hidden + service-set)
+kubebuilder:validation:Required

NodePoolSpecPassthrough.description: NodePoolSpecPassthrough mirrors NodePoolSpec from upstream HyperShift

NodePoolStatus.description: NodePoolStatus defines the observed state of a NodePool

availableReplicas.description: AvailableReplicas is the number of available nodes

conditions.description: Conditions represent detailed node pool status

readyReplicas.description: ReadyReplicas is the number of ready nodes

replicas.description: Replicas is the current number of nodes

state.description:
State represents the high-level node pool state
+kubebuilder:validation:Enum=pending;scaling;ready;degraded;deleting;failed

SystemdDropin.description: SystemdDropin represents a systemd drop-in configuration.

contents.description: contents is the drop-in file contents

name.description: name is the name of the drop-in file

SystemdUnit.description: SystemdUnit represents a systemd unit configuration.

contents.description: contents is the full systemd unit file contents

dropins.description: dropins are drop-in configurations for the unit

enabled.description: enabled specifies whether the unit is enabled

name.description: name is the name of the systemd unit (e.g., "custom.service")
